fixes.reason: T031 ARR1 KO predicted decreased but biology says increased
fixes.biological_justification: Per E014 mechanism: Type-B ARR1 is a negative regulator of branching; arr1 mutant has increased branching (Nguyen-Emery 2017 review)

fixes.reason: Modifier 1.0 = WT, not OE. GOF should be 2.0
fixes.biological_justification: Standard OE encoding per BUILDER spec

fixes.reason: Treatment should push environmental input above baseline

fixes.description: T057 max2+GR24 - added MAX2 gm=0, baseline=mutant, expected=unchanged
fixes.reason: Trap 5 (signaling mutant rescue) - max2 receptor KO blocks SL response
fixes.biological_justification: Per BUILDER Â§14 TRAP 5: Signaling/receptor mutant + exogenous hormone = unchanged (NOT rescued)

fixes.description: T059 brc1 brc2 +GR24 - added BRC1 gm=0, baseline=mutant, expected=unchanged
fixes.reason: BRC1 downstream of SL; KO blocks SL pathway effect
fixes.biological_justification: BRC1 is the SL signaling output node; without BRC1, SL cannot affect branching via this cascade

fixes.description: T096 d14+GR24 - added D14 gm=0, baseline=mutant, expected=unchanged
fixes.biological_justification: D14 receptor KO blocks SL response

fixes.description: T142 max3 KO expected unchanged -> increased
fixes.reason: max3 (CCD7) is the canonical SL biosynthesis overbranching mutant
fixes.biological_justification: Stirnberg 2002, Booker 2004 - max3 mutants show strongly increased shoot branching

fixes.description: T132 SL biosynthesis mutant + GR24 expected increased -> decreased
fixes.reason: Biosynthesis mutant rescue: exo hormone restores WT-like phenotype, baseline=mutant
fixes.biological_justification: Per BUILDER encoding rules: biosynthesis mutant + hormone = decreased from mutant baseline
